ground_truth.title: How I assembled my own Plasma desktop
ground_truth.main_content: This guide details assembling a Plasma desktop on ARM architecture using Ubuntu Server. The author installed Ubuntu Server on a Macbook Pro with Apple Silicon (ARM), then added the Plasma desktop environment using kde-plasma-desktop package. Several issues were encountered and resolved: Discover app showed offline status due to NetworkManager not being properly configured (solved by creating a netplan configuration to defer network management to NetworkManager), and VLC had no audio output due to missing Pipewire support and incomplete ALSA configuration (solved by installing alsa-utils and pointing VLC to ALSA output). The final system includes multiple KDE applications, browsers (Firefox, Thunderbird), productivity tools, and works well despite the non-standard setup. The author emphasizes that with Ubuntu Server image, users can build their own desktop system even when pre-built options aren't available.
with: I downloaded the ISO, configured a virtual machine (through VirtualBox), went through a quick text-based installer, and soon thereafter, my headless Ubuntu server was ready.
Luckily, there's a single meta package that allows you to download the entire Plasma desktop from the Ubuntu repositories, with one command.
Discover uses PackageKit, which checks and polls NetworkManager for connections. Now, the GUI NetworkManager didn't have any listed, not even the loopback device.
The solution is to disable the systemd component that manages the network, and then tell netplan to defer the control and management of the network interfaces to NetworkManager.
VLC does not seem to have a default Pipewire audio output, but since alsa and pulseaudio aren't fully configured on this server build, there's no sound.
